name: Siqi [Edward] Guo
about:
I am a CMU M.S. ECE graduate (2025) currently working as a **Robot Intern at Yondu AI** (Y Combinator-backed humanoid robotics startup), where I develop parallelized C++ inverse kinematics and motion planning pipelines for wheeled humanoid warehouse robots.

My research interests center on **robot learning and control systems** — particularly real-time robot learning, embodied AI, world models for robotics, and scalable deployment of foundation models on physical platforms.

Previously, I was an **Embedded Software Engineer at Cepton** (LiDAR systems), where I built PD control algorithms with feedforward for precision mirror control, redesigned SPI/event-queue architecture to cut CPU utilization by 73%, and engineered a real-time debug system in Rust.

At CMU, I was a Research Assistant in the **Biorobotics Lab** under Professor Howie Choset, contributing to **EigenBot** (bio-inspired distributed locomotion controller, published at ICRA 2025) and **MMPUG** (autonomous multi-agent planning with A*/Theta* global planner on real RC cars).

I hold a B.S. in Computer Science from Tianjin University (2023), where I published work on adversarial robustness of GNNs at **AAAI 2023** and in the **IEEE Journal of Neural Networks**.
